sections.text: Last updated: 18 June 2026

These Terms of Service govern your use of the Nava Hatha Yoga website and your participation in our in-person classes, programs, and events. By using this website or registering for a program, you agree to these terms.
About Nava Hatha Yoga
All classes and programs are taught in person unless otherwise stated on the website or in event information.
Use of this website
You may use this website for lawful purposes only. You agree not to misuse the site, attempt to gain unauthorised access to our systems, or submit false or misleading information through our forms.
Programs, events, and registration

Program descriptions, schedules, locations, and prices shown on the website are provided in good faith and may be updated. Registration through the website does not, by itself, confirm your place in a program. We will contact you to confirm availability, payment, and any further details.
By registering, you confirm that the information you provide is accurate and complete to the best of your knowledge.
Health and participation

Classical Hatha Yoga involves physical activity. You are responsible for deciding whether a program is appropriate for you. Please consult a qualified health professional before beginning any new practice if you have a medical condition, injury, are pregnant, or have any concern about your ability to participate safely.
You agree to follow the teacher's guidance, practise within your limits, and inform us of any relevant health information requested during registration.
Payments and cancellations

Payment terms, refund conditions, and cancellation policies for specific programs or events are communicated at the time of registration and in any confirmation correspondence. Unless otherwise stated, fees are for in-person instruction and related guidance as described for each offering.
Intellectual property

Content on this website — including text, images, layout, and branding — is owned by Nava Hatha Yoga or used with permission. You may not copy, reproduce, or distribute website content for commercial purposes without our prior written consent.
Limitation of liability

To the fullest extent permitted by applicable law, Nava Hatha Yoga is not liable for any indirect or consequential loss arising from your use of this website or participation in our programs, except where liability cannot be excluded by law.
Changes to these terms

We may update these Terms of Service from time to time. The updated version will be posted on this page with a revised date. Continued use of the website after changes are published constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.
Contact
If you have questions about these terms, please contact us at info@navahathayoga.com.
Last updated: 18 June 2026
This Privacy Policy explains how Nava Hatha Yoga collects, uses, and protects personal information when you use our website, contact us, or register for a program.
Who we are
Nava Hatha Yoga offers Classical Hatha Yoga. For privacy-related enquiries, contact us at info@navahathayoga.com.
Information we collect
We collect only the information you choose to provide, including:
Contact form: your name, email address, phone number, optional program or interest details, and your message.
Registration form: personal and contact details, emergency contact information, health-related information you provide, and other details needed to prepare for your participation.
Technical data: basic server and security logs may be processed by our hosting provider when you visit the website.
How we use your information
We use your information to:

Respond to enquiries and communicate with you about programs and events; process registrations and prepare for your participation; send administrative messages related to your booking; and maintain the security and proper functioning of our website.
We do not sell your personal information.
Legal basis for processing

Where applicable under data protection law, we process your information on the basis of your consent (for example, when you submit a form), our legitimate interest in responding to enquiries and organising programs, and/or steps taken at your request before entering into an agreement for a class or program.
How long we keep information
We retain personal information only for as long as needed for the purposes described above, including any period required for legal, accounting, or safety-related record-keeping.
Sharing your information

We may share information with service providers who help us operate the website and deliver email notifications (such as our hosting and email delivery providers). These providers process data only on our instructions and for the purposes described in this policy.
We may also disclose information if required by law or to protect the rights, safety, and security of participants, teachers, or others.
Your rights

Depending on applicable law, you may have the right to request access to, correction of, or deletion of your personal information, or to object to or restrict certain processing. To exercise these rights, contact us at info@navahathayoga.com.
Security

We take reasonable steps to protect personal information from unauthorised access, loss, or misuse. No method of transmission over the internet is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security.
Changes to this policy
We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. The current version will always be available on this page with the date of the latest revision.
Contact
For privacy questions or requests, email info@navahathayoga.com.
Last updated: 18 June 2026
This Cookie Policy explains how Nava Hatha Yoga uses cookies and similar technologies when you visit our website.
What are cookies?
Cookies are small text files stored on your device when you visit a website. They help the site function properly and can remember basic preferences.
How we use cookies
We aim to keep cookie use minimal. Our website is designed to rely on essential cookies and similar technologies needed for security, load balancing, and basic site operation.
We do not use advertising cookies or sell data collected through cookies.
Third-party services

Some embedded content — such as YouTube videos shown on certain pages — may set cookies when you interact with that content. Those cookies are controlled by the third-party provider and are subject to their own policies.
Managing cookies
You can control or delete cookies through your browser settings. Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect how parts of the website work.
Changes to this policy
If our use of cookies changes, we will update this page. Material changes may also be reflected in our Privacy Policy where relevant.
Contact
If you have questions about this Cookie Policy, contact us at info@navahathayoga.com.
